chief_complaint: A 50-year-old woman presented to the emergency department with acute gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: Gastrointestinal stromal tumours (GISTs) are rare mesenchymal neoplasms. The patient presented with a painless mass in the right lower abdomen and a large, lobulated mass in the left flank/iliac fossa measuring 15x16x8.5 cm on CT.
exam: Physical examination revealed a painless mass in the right lower abdomen. Vital signs were not provided.
labs: CT scan: large, lobulated mass in the left flank/iliac fossa measuring 15x16x8.5 cm. Exploratory laparotomy: tumour invading two segments of the small intestine. Histopathological evaluation: GIST (c-Kit and DOG1 positive).
